skills.name: Pipeline Audit Methodology
skills.description: Framework for conducting systematic pipeline audits
skills.content:
PIPELINE AUDIT FRAMEWORK:
1. Stage Distribution: Count deals per stage, calculate % of total pipeline value in each
2. Velocity Analysis: Average days in each stage, compare against benchmarks (Discovery: 7-14d, Demo: 5-10d, Proposal: 7-14d, Negotiation: 10-21d)
3. Conversion Rates: Stage-to-stage conversion, flag any below 30% as bottleneck
4. Stale Deal Detection: Flag deals with no activity >14 days, no stage change >30 days
5. Pipeline Coverage: Total pipeline / quota target, healthy = 3-4x coverage
6. Deal Quality Score: Weight by stage probability (Discovery 10%, Demo 25%, Proposal 50%, Negotiation 75%, Verbal 90%)
7. Risk Flags: Single-threaded deals, missing close dates, no next steps logged

skills.name: Revenue Forecasting Model
skills.description: Weighted pipeline forecasting with historical calibration
skills.content:
REVENUE FORECASTING METHOD:
1. Weighted Pipeline: Sum of (deal value × stage probability) for all active deals
2. Historical Calibration: Compare weighted forecast against last 3 months actual close rates by stage — adjust probabilities if actuals differ >10%
3. Commit vs Best Case vs Upside: Commit = deals in Negotiation+ with confirmed timeline. Best Case = Commit + high-probability Proposal deals. Upside = Best Case + qualified Discovery deals
4. Trend Analysis: Month-over-month pipeline creation rate, average deal size trend, win rate trend
5. Risk Adjustment: Subtract 15-20% from Best Case for deals with: no champion identified, competitor present, budget not confirmed
6. Output: Single-number forecast with confidence interval (±range based on historical variance)

skills.name: Tech Stack Assessment
skills.description: Framework for evaluating and recommending RevOps tool stacks
skills.content:
TECH STACK ASSESSMENT FRAMEWORK:
1. Current State Mapping: List all tools across Sales, Marketing, CS, Finance with integration status
2. Data Flow Audit: Trace how a lead becomes a customer — every system touch, manual handoff, and data gap
3. Integration Health: Score each integration (native/API/Zapier/manual) — manual = red flag
4. Redundancy Check: Identify overlapping tools doing the same job
5. Gap Analysis: Map against canonical RevOps stack (CRM, Marketing Automation, Sales Engagement, CPQ, CS Platform, BI/Analytics, Data Warehouse)
6. ROI Scoring: For each recommended change — estimated cost, implementation time, expected efficiency gain
7. Priority Matrix: Plot recommendations on effort vs impact 2x2

skills.name: Client Deliverable Standards
skills.description: Formatting and structure standards for all client-facing outputs
skills.content:
CLIENT DELIVERABLE STANDARDS:
- Always start with a 3-sentence Executive Summary that a CRO can read in 30 seconds
- Use specific numbers everywhere — never say 'significant' when you can say '34%'
- Structure: Executive Summary → Key Findings (3-5 bullets) → Detailed Analysis → Recommendations → Next Steps
- Each recommendation must include: What to do, Who owns it, Timeline, Expected impact
- Use comparison framing: 'Your 28% demo-to-proposal rate is below the 40% B2B SaaS median'
- End with exactly 3 prioritized next steps with deadlines
- Tone: Confident, direct, advisory — like a partner at McKinsey, not a vendor

skills.name: CRM Data Quality Checklist
skills.description: Standard checks for CRM data hygiene
skills.content:
DATA QUALITY CHECKLIST:
1. Completeness: % of deals missing close date, amount, stage, owner, contact
2. Freshness: % of contacts with no activity logged in 90+ days
3. Duplicates: Flag contacts/companies appearing multiple times
4. Stage Integrity: Deals that skipped stages or went backward without explanation
5. Owner Balance: Deals per rep — flag >2 standard deviations from mean
6. Amount Accuracy: Deals with $0 or suspiciously round numbers ($100,000 exactly)
7. Activity Coverage: % of open deals with at least 1 logged activity in last 14 days
8. Scoring: Grade overall hygiene A-F, benchmark against: A(>90%), B(75-90%), C(60-75%), D(<60%)
